ddls:
CREATE TABLE "streaming_services" (
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each streaming service */,
  "service_name" TEXT /* Name of the streaming service */,
  "pricing_model" TEXT /* Pricing model (e.g., subscription, ad-supported) */,
  "subscription_fee" REAL /* Monthly subscription fee for the service */,
  "launch_year" INTEGER /* Year the service was launched */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the streaming service */,
  "logo_url" TEXT /* URL to the service's logo */,
  "website_url" TEXT /* URL to the service's official website */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the service is primarily available */,
  "languages_supported" TEXT /* Languages supported by the service */,
  "customer_support" TEXT /* Contact information for customer support */,
  "device_compatibility" TEXT /* Devices on which the service is available */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "channels" (
  "channel_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each channel */,
  "channel_name" TEXT /* Name of the channel */,
  "genre" TEXT /* Genre of the channel (e.g., entertainment, news) */,
  "content_type" TEXT /* Type of content broadcasted by the channel (e.g., live, recorded) */,
  "logo_url" TEXT /* URL to the channel's logo */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the channel is primarily available */,
  "languages_supported" TEXT /* Languages in which the channel broadcasts */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("channel_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_channels" (
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the streaming service */,
  "channel_id" INTEGER /* ID of the channel */,
  "availability" TEXT /* Availability of the channel on the service (e.g., ✔, None) */,
  "price" REAL /* Price of the channel if it is an add-on */,
  "hd_support" INTEGER /* Whether the channel supports HD */,
  "dvr_support" INTEGER /* Whether the channel supports DVR */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_id", "channel_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_profiles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "preferred_service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user's preferred streaming service */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* User's first name */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* User's last name */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* User's date of birth */,
  "preferences" TEXT /* JSON column to store user preferences */,
  "subscription_status" INTEGER /* Whether the user is subscribed to the preferred service */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_profiles_preferred_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("preferred_service_id") REFERENCES streaming_services ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ratings_reviews" (
  "review_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each review */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who submitted the review */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the streaming service being reviewed */,
  "rating" REAL /* Rating given by the user (1-5) */,
  "review_text" TEXT /* Text of the review provided by the user */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date when the review was submitted */,
  "helpful_votes" INTEGER /* Number of users who found the review helpful */,
  "verified_user" INTEGER /* Whether the user is verified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("review_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_reviews_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES user_profiles ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_reviews_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES streaming_services ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "promotions" (
  "promo_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each promotion */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated streaming service */,
  "promo_description" TEXT /* Description of the promotional offer */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the promotion */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the promotion */,
  "discount" REAL /* Percentage or amount of discount */,
  "promotion_code" TEXT /* Code to be used for the promotion */,
  "terms_conditions" TEXT /* Terms and conditions of the promotion */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("promo_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_promotions_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES streaming_services ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analytics_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytics entry */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the streaming service accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who accessed the service */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the service was accessed */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., view, click) */,
  "session_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the user's session */,
  "device" TEXT /* Device used to access the service */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytics_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES user_profiles ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES streaming_services ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "content_types" (
  "content_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each content type */,
  "content_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the content type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the content type */,
  "icon_url" TEXT /* URL to an icon representing the content type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("content_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genres" (
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each genre */,
  "genre_name" TEXT /* Name of the genre */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the genre */,
  "icon_url" TEXT /* URL to an icon representing the genre */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_features" (
  "feature_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feature */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated streaming service */,
  "feature_name" TEXT /* Name of the feature (e.g., DVR, Offline Viewing) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the feature */,
  "icon_url" TEXT /* URL to an icon representing the feature */,
  "is_premium" INTEGER /* Whether the feature is part of a premium plan */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feature_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_features_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES streaming_services ("service_id")
)
